name: Doneztebe, Sunbilla, Etxalar, Arizkun, Elizondo, Oronoz – Circular Route
name_original: Doneztebe, Sunbilla, Etxalar, Arizkun, Elizondo, Oronoz – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around Doneztebe/Santesteban offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and river valleys in the Navarre region. The area features a network of trails that traverse both natural and cultural points of interest. Elevations vary, providing options from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents. This region is suitable for various hiking abilities, with routes exploring the local terrain. Best hiking trails around Doneztebe/Santesteban The most popular hiking route is Water Route / Water Path loop from Doneztebe / Santesteban, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate circular walking route follows the historic water path, offering a scenic experience. Another top favourite among local hikers is Casa Torre de Donamaria loop from Donamaria, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This route features a significant elevation gain of over 1,200 feet (389 meters) and passes by historical architecture. Local hikers also love the Oteixon Summit – Small Grove loop from Sunbilla, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 3 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Doneztebe/Santesteban is defined by forested hills, river paths, and elevated viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy hikes Doneztebe/Santesteban and more challenging ascents. The routes in Doneztebe/Santesteban are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 2,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Doneztebe/Santesteban's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Doneztebe/Santesteban?
A: There are over 80 hiking routes in the Doneztebe/Santesteban area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Doneztebe/Santesteban offers 12 easy hiking routes. A good option for a gentle walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20516933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Route / Water Path loop from Doneztebe / Santesteban&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.6 km (4.7 miles) trail with minimal elevation gain, suitable for most abilities.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Doneztebe/Santesteban?
A: The region features diverse terrain, including rolling hills, dense forests, and river valleys. You'll find paths ranging from gentle riverside strolls to more challenging ascents through wooded areas, offering varied landscapes to explore.

Q: Are there any circular walking routes in the area?
A: Many of the trails around Doneztebe/Santesteban are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31881060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Torre de Donamaria loop from Donamaria&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route covering 8.9 km (5.5 miles).

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can enjoy scenic viewpoints from higher elevations, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15004062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oteixon Summit – Small Grove loop from Sunbilla&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features natural landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1098435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur Summit and Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, which can be reached via more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10518564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;MenhirDeLearra – Mendaur Summit and Hermitage loop from Sunbilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit while hiking?
A: Yes, the region is home to beautiful waterfalls. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5840990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bisusto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3604192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Putzubeltza Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, both offering picturesque natural sights along or near hiking paths.

Q: What other attractions can I explore near Doneztebe/Santesteban besides hiking?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore cultural and natural attractions such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3860943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doneztebe (Santesteban) Historic Centre&lt;/a&gt;, the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4017261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etxalar Village&lt;/a&gt;, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Xar Cave Shrine&lt;/a&gt;. Nature lovers might also enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3860957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oronoz and Señorío de Bertiz Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Doneztebe/Santesteban?
A: The hiking routes in Doneztebe/Santesteban are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, well-maintained paths, and the peaceful natural surroundings.

Q: Is Doneztebe/Santesteban a good destination for experienced hikers looking for a challenge?
A: Absolutely. While there are many easy and moderate options, Doneztebe/Santesteban also offers 23 difficult routes. These trails feature significant elevation gains and longer distances, providing a rewarding challenge for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any specific considerations for hiking in winter in Doneztebe/Santesteban?
A: While the region is generally accessible year-round, winter hiking may involve colder temperatures and potentially wet or muddy conditions, especially in forested areas. Higher elevations might see occasional snow. Always check local weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out during winter months.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the hiking trails?
A: Doneztebe/Santesteban and its surrounding villages offer various local establishments where you can find refreshments and meals. Many routes start or pass through these towns, making it convenient to combine your hike with a stop at a cafe or restaurant.
tips: Beautiful and picturesque town next to the Ezkurra river with all the services you need. Some of the places to visit if you pass through here are the San Pedro Eliza Church, the main square and the water walk along the river.
The Viaje Verde runs along the Bidasoa. This is dammed at regular intervals.
A 14th century wash house in Sunbilla.

In the historic town of Sunbilla you can find its centuries-old stone houses on both sides of the Bidasoa, but they are joined by a historic stone bridge. Before building the stone bridge, there was already a wooden one, but it was frequently washed away by the flooding of the river.
Along the Bidassoa green way, across the river, there is this beautiful waterfall.
Former railway tunnels of the Viaje Verde (EV1).
It is smaller and less noisy.

Known for the beauty of its houses, Etxalar is a pretty village in the Basque Country surrounded by mountains and forests. Wood pigeon hunting is one of the traditions that reinforces its authentic culture.

A small town where you can admire the Palace of the armory of the Ursúa, the Church of San Juan Bautista and the Baroque Convent of Our Lady of the Angels. Every year they have an ancestral carnival in which the main character is the "hartza" (bear in Basque).

Oronoz is the gateway to the natural park and has a "zaharra garden", which brings together 126 species of trees and plants. The rest of the area has Atlantic forest with beech forests, and has various marked trails for trips on foot, by bicycle or on horseback. It is also recommended to go up to the Aizkolegi palace

waypoints.name: Oronoz and Señorío de Bertiz Natural Park
